# Platform Policy Boundary Comparison

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

| Platform / regulator | Boundary phrase | What it protects |
|---|---|---|
| FTC | Fake reviews/testimonials and fake social-media indicators | Consumer trust and fair competition |
| ACCC | Paid or gifted influencer content must not mislead | Consumer interpretation of reviews/promotions |
| YouTube | No artificial increases to views, likes, comments, metrics | Metric integrity |
| TikTok | No fake engagement, bulk account manipulation, or recommendation deception | Recommendation integrity and authentic accounts |
| X | No coordinated inauthentic activity or compensated metric inflation | Conversation and discovery integrity |
| Meta | Reward original content; deprioritize unoriginal/copycat content | Original creator visibility |
| LinkedIn | Engagement pods and automated comments erode trust | Professional credibility |
| GitHub | No rank abuse such as automated starring/following | Open-source trust signals |
| Google Maps | Reviews must reflect genuine experience; no incentivized fake engagement | Local business trust |
| Google Play | No manipulation of app placement via ratings, reviews, installs | App discovery integrity |
| Product Hunt | No mass upvote requests, bots, or incentivized voting | Launch ranking integrity |
| OECD | Dark patterns steer, deceive, coerce, or manipulate choices | Consumer autonomy |

## Key synthesis

The platforms differ, but the boundary is consistent: fake identity, fake engagement, fake reviews, fake independence, fake originality, hidden coordination, and deceptive choice architecture undermine trust infrastructure.
